title: Cyclist/pedestrian collision avoidance system
abstract: Bicycle trips account for a disproportionate number of fatalities in the United States. In an embodiment, a method can include calculating a proximity, velocity, and direction of a cyclist relative to a vehicle. The proximity, velocity, and direction are relative measures of a car to a bicycle. The calculation may be based on a wireless signal received from a wireless transmitter associated with the bicycle at a wireless receiver associated with the car. The method may further includes providing an alert to an operator of the vehicle based on the calculated proximity, velocity, and direction. In this manner, the vehicle driver can be alerted to the bicycle and avoid a collision. Both cyclists and drivers receive periodic safety reports on the smartphone about their riding habits. The smartphone is used alone or in conjunction of a separate device.
